What is the sum of the largest and the smallest 4-digit numbers made by using single digit prime numbers (without repetition)?

Q25 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Conflict misses can be reduced by increasing value of cache associativity.
S2 : On increasing block size of cache, miss penalty also increases.
S3 : Increasing in block size of cache always decrease miss rate.
Which of the above statements are false?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
On continuous increase in block size leads to less cache lines. And it increases the competition for cache lines, which can result as high miss rate. Also, if block is too big then on a miss it replace block of size greater than spatial locality and unnecessary locations loaded in cache. If block size is large, then replacement will also take more time, so high miss penalty. Small increment in block size can be beneficial. But, after a limit, it will lead to high miss rate. And, question mentioned always.

Q28 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) The pointer overhead for linked allocation is greater than index allocation.
(b) Demand paging requires the programmer to instruct the operating system to load a particular virtual memory page.
(c) Pages that shares between two or more process can never be swapped out to the disk.
(d) Context of the process is useful to restart the process after sometimes.
Which of the above is/are incorrect?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
(a) The pointer overhead for index allocation is greater than linked allocation. So, option (a) is incorrect.
(b) The operating system automatically loads pages from disk when necessary when it is needed.
So, option (b) is incorrect.
(c) Pages that shares between two or more process can be swapped out to disk when demand paging is applied and we have to swap in new pages and main memory is full. So, option (c) is incorrect.
(d) Content of the process is useful to restart the process after sometimes. So, option (d) is correct.

Q33 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The less-than relation, <, on a set of real numbers is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Relation less than a set of real numbers is not antisymmetric and reflexive. Relation is not POSET because it is irreflexive. Again, aRb != bRa unless a = b and so it is antisymmetric. A relation may be not asymmetric and not reflexive but still antisymmetric, as {(1, 1) (1, 2)}. So, the relation is not a partial ordering because it is not asymmetric and irreflexive equals antisymmetric.

Q36 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• LALR(1) is less powerful than CLR(1). So, option (a) is correct.
• If a grammar is LR(0), it should be SLR(1). So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Control link in an activation record points to be the activation record of the caller. So, option (c) is correct.
• Data segment storage has 2 nodes read only and read-write. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q40 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

For a class C network if IP address of a computer is 200.99.39.112 and subnet mask is 255.255.255.224 the decimal value of last octet of last host of sixth subnet is __________.

NAT
Correct answer: 222
Solution

222
Since the subnet mask is 255.255.255.224 that means the first 3 bits of subnet are reserved for subnet id and rest 5 are host id bits.
Hence, sixth subnet, last octet will be [11011110]2 = [222]10.

Q48 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Suppose that you are using TCP over a 109 bps (1 Gbits/s) link with 1-way latency of 100 ms to transfer a 2 * 106 (2 MB) byte file and the TCP receive window size is always 106 (1 MB) bytes. If TCP sends 103 (1 KB) packets: (ignore packet transmission time and assume no packet loss)

How many RTTs does it take until slow start opens the sender’s window to 1 MB?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
One approach is to figure out the how much data is sent in each round trip. Given we know the 1-way latency of each RTT is 200 ms. If we can compute the data sent as a function of the RTT, and we know the time per RTT, we can compute the throughput, i.e. bytes/time. The TCP slow-start algorithm doubles the window, measured in segments, each RTT (until we reach linear mode, which we can ignore in this question). The send window exceeds 106 at the end of the 10th RTT.

Q58 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true?
I. Selection of pivot element decides the time complexity of quick sort.
II. Applying binary search for comparison in insertion sort does not reduces the time complexity.
III. Suppose there are n elements where the minimum element is a 4 digit number. Then radix sort will take 4 iterations to sort all the elements.
IV. Suppose there are n elements in the range [1 to n2]. Then counting sort performs better than selection sort in the worst case.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(d)
III is incorrect as iterations radix sort is dependent on the length of the maximum element.
IV is incorrect as for the given range of elements, counting sort time complexity will be same as selection sort time complexity in worst case.

Q65 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements and identify the correct statements:

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
• Conflict misses in fact will increase by increasing block size because there will be less number of lines. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• Larger block reduce compulsory misses by improving spatial locality. So, option (b) is correct.
• In synchronous clock is common and in asynchronous clock is different.
